technicalSummary: I work at the intersection of design and engineering, owning product problems from Figma to production code without handing off between the two.

My background is DeFi and blockchain infrastructure, which means I bring technical depth to domains most product designers approach from the outside. At Neptune Finance I built the design function from scratch: custom Figma component library, full design system, and every line of production TypeScript and Tailwind, while maintaining the engineering rigour required for live financial systems where on-chain state, wallet behaviour, and transaction reliability are non-negotiable.

I use AI tools (Cursor, Claude, and agentic workflows) as a core part of how I prototype and ship. I've directed fully agentic engineering flows, maintaining product and design ownership while accelerating implementation significantly.

coreCompetencies.description: AI tools are a core part of how I prototype and ship, from directing agentic implementation flows to integrating AI into design iteration and code review.

projects.title: Neptune Finance
projects.roleDescriptor: Design Engineer / Frontend Lead. Full product ownership from Figma to production
projects.content: Frontend lead for Neptune Finance, a lending and borrowing system on Injective. Built the application layer that keeps contract state, wallet state, and UI state synchronized during deposits, borrows, repayments, and reward flows.
projects.content2: Implemented contract-facing features across staking, airdrop, and liquidity routing. Production flows include Mito vault integrations and Astroport pool interactions with explicit handling for transaction states, retries, and confirmation boundaries.
projects.content3: Owned the frontend system architecture in Next.js and TypeScript, including reusable UI primitives, data aggregation from chain and indexing services, and feature gating linked to on-chain staking eligibility.
scope: Full product ownership: Figma design system through production TypeScript
Custom Figma component library and visual design language
UX architecture for lending, staking, and liquidity flows
Full frontend architecture and engineering systems
Contract and wallet integration surfaces
Smart contract integrations (Mito, Astroport)
Staking, airdrop, and liquidity systems
context: Neptune Finance is a DeFi lending protocol on Injective with CosmWasm contracts.
The frontend coordinates wallet actions, contract state, and market data to keep lending and liquidity flows correct during live transaction execution.
problemSpace: On-chain and UI state can drift during pending transactions; users making financial decisions need accurate state at all times
Market-sensitive screens require low-latency updates and interfaces designed for fast decision-making
Multi-step flows across lending, staking, and liquidity must feel simple despite significant underlying contract complexity
Wallet signing and broadcast failures need explicit recovery paths; confusion at this moment breaks user trust and risks funds
approach: Model transaction lifecycle states as first-class UI states
Use reusable query and component primitives for protocol modules
Align user actions to contract message boundaries
Provide deterministic error and retry handling around wallet operations
outcomes: Shipped lending, staking, airdrop, and liquidity workflows to production users with real funds at stake
Improved transaction reliability and reduced user confusion through explicit state handling and recovery UX
Built a design system that kept Figma components and production code in sync across a fast-moving codebase
Identified and shipped the balance menu independently: no brief, designed in Figma, implemented in production

projects.title: stakefish / f2pool
projects.content: Produced technical protocol education assets for stakefish and f2pool, translating validator and staking operations into repeatable user workflows across Ethereum, Cosmos, and Solana.
projects.content2: Work prioritized procedural correctness and chain-specific detail so onboarding content matched real wallet and network steps used by operators and delegators.
projects.content3: Contributed protocol communication support during infrastructure and consensus transitions, including Ethereum's move to Proof-of-Stake.

projects.stack: Video Production, Figma, Protocol Documentation, YouTube API
scope: Created instructional visual media translating validator operations into repeatable workflows
Supported protocol updates and network transitions with developer-focused materials
Built content highlighting EVM and Cosmos ecosystem validation procedures
context: Translating technical validator node settings and staking details into visual, error-free step-by-step guides for users and operators.
Ensuring compliance with chain-specific details so onboarding content matches real-world wallet operations.
problemSpace: Technical density of staking protocols causes user mistakes during delegate/undelegate transactions
Chain-specific nuances (Ethereum vs. Solana vs. Cosmos) require highly accurate documentation
approach: Model validator onboarding flows based on real network and wallet steps
Standardize visual workflows and annotations using Figma and clear diagrams
outcomes: Shipped validator training materials for Osmosis, Evmos, Ledger, and Beacon Chain workflows
Handled official protocol messaging during Ethereum's historic transition to Proof-of-Stake

projects.title: Open Source Contributions
projects.content: Led integration work to bring CW3 Fixed Multisig support to Injective by refactoring Cosmos ecosystem frontend code for Injective execution paths.
projects.content2: Refactored transaction submission to align with Injective SDK, improved execution diagnostics through Base64 message decoding, and added local caching for contract-heavy workflows.
projects.content3: Contributed protocol data and adapters to Cosmos Chain Registry, Injective Labs, Keplr Contract Registry, and DeFi Llama.
projects.stack: TypeScript, React, Keplr, Injective SDK, Caching
scope: Ported and refactored multisig frontend infrastructure to Injective chain paths
Handled CosmWasm contract interfaces and Keplr wallet integrations
Implemented transaction diagnostics and decoding
Contributed protocol data and adapters to core registries
context: CW3 fixed multisig dApps lacked robust Injective support and chain-specific transaction serialization.
Refactoring was needed to bring this infrastructure to Injective.
problemSpace: Incompatible transaction paths with standard Cosmos SDK chains
Hard-to-debug transaction failures with encoded base64 contract messages
Caching and performance issues for heavy contract query workloads
approach: Refactor submission pipeline to utilize the Injective SDK
Add Base64 decoding for in-browser message verification
Leverage query-level caching to avoid rate-limiting on node queries
outcomes: Delivered functional CW3 multisig interface for Injective users
Contributed vital chain registration adapters back to Keystone/Keplr registries and DeFi Llama

projects.title: Cryptech Developments Ltd.
projects.content: Built and maintained the Cryptech web platform and frontend systems for associated product surfaces, including Macro DEX interface work.
projects.content2: Focused on reusable frontend implementation in React/Next.js and component-level consistency across company and product properties.
scope: Built company landing pages and developer platform dashboard
Maintained reusable CSS style guide and UI component library
Integrated Macro DEX exchange interfaces
context: Cryptech Developments required a centralized platform dashboard and corporate website that shared components and brand styling.
problemSpace: Component fragmentation and styling drift between company marketing sites and the developer dashboard
Responsive alignment and asset packaging for low-latency dashboard operations
approach: Create shared Tailwind configurations and custom CSS utility layer
Wrap interactive states into clean, reusable React components
outcomes: Unified developer dashboard and platform sites with shared style guidelines
Reduced component development time for new features by implementing design tokens

projects.title: All About Blockchain
projects.content: Co-founded All About Blockchain, focused on node infrastructure and ecosystem product development.
projects.content2: Contributed to Stakefolio, a staking portfolio tracking application with wallet-linked data views and multi-network portfolio context.
projects.stack: TypeScript, React, Next.js, Figma, Tailwind, Vercel
scope: Co-founded node validation infrastructure company
Designed and built Stakefolio, a multi-network staking tracker
Integrated wallet interactions and aggregated network telemetry
context: All About Blockchain was founded to operate secure validator infrastructure while delivering simple interfaces for staking portfolio management.
problemSpace: Users delegating tokens across multiple networks lack a unified visual overview of their rewards and status
Maintaining correct portfolio state across different RPC interfaces (Cosmos, Injective) is highly complex
approach: Build a multi-chain dashboard using Next.js to aggregate staking indices
Design visual dashboard layouts directly in Figma and port them to Tailwind utility classes
outcomes: Successfully launched node operations on Cosmos and Injective ecosystems
Delivered Stakefolio tracking tools for managing multi-chain delegation profiles

projects.title: Cosmoverse 2023 / Hackathons
projects.content: Built a dynamic event scheduling interface for Cosmoverse 2023 with externalized schedule data for fast operational updates.
projects.content2: Contributed to hackathon systems including Dyve (NFT lending/renting/shorting primitive) and Turing.Bet (wallet-connected prediction workflow).
projects.content3: Satsuma implemented a cross-chain yield routing system: USDC flow on Injective, yield skimming and swap through Astroport, bridge handoff via Axelar GMP, and Bitcoin conversion orchestration on ICP.

ctaPanelOverrides.description: Case studies & shipping scope: cross-chain yield routing UI (Injective, Astroport, Axelar GMP, ICP).

projects.stack: React, TypeScript, Astroport, Axelar GMP, Injective, ICP
scope: Designed and built the user interface for Satsuma yield routing
Handled multi-chain wallet status and status transitions
Implemented yield accounting calculations and route visualization
context: Built during the Cosmoverse hackathon to automate the conversion of yield to Bitcoin across multiple chains.
Shipped at Cosmoverse hackathon operating in a fully agentic engineering flow, directing implementation through chat while maintaining product and design ownership throughout.
problemSpace: High friction in cross-chain UX due to slow state handoffs between Injective and ICP
Yield accounting complexity across multiple pools and protocols
Wallet state syncing across multiple networks simultaneously
approach: Model cross-chain transition stages as an animated, step-by-step progress tracker
Aggregate Astroport pool rates and ICP canister states on the client side
outcomes: Shipped the full working prototype under a fast hackathon schedule
Demonstrated cross-chain yield routing with Astroport and Axelar GMP integrations

projects.title: Satsuma
projects.roleDescriptor: Design Engineer / Frontend Lead. Cross-chain yield-to-Bitcoin conversion routing built at Cosmoverse Hackathon
projects.content: Design engineer and frontend lead for Satsuma, a cross-chain yield routing and yield-to-Bitcoin conversion platform built during the Cosmoverse Hackathon.
projects.content2: Satsuma orchestrates a multi-network yield optimization pipeline: routing USDC deposits on Injective, executing yield skimming and automated token swaps via Astroport pools, coordinating cross-chain messaging using Axelar GMP, and executing Bitcoin purchases via canisters on the Internet Computer Protocol (ICP).
projects.content3: Designed and implemented a real-time cross-chain transition tracker visualizing exact deposit, bridge, and swap states, and integrated multiple wallet protocols (Keplr, OIS, Metamask, ICP Internet Identity) into a single client interface. Shipped operating in a fully agentic engineering flow, directing implementation through chat while maintaining complete product and design ownership.
projects.stack: React, TypeScript, Astroport, Axelar GMP, Injective, ICP
scope: Designed the multi-chain dashboard and transaction tracker in Figma
Implemented the Injective contract interactions and wallet state sync (Keplr)
Developed Axelar General Message Passing (GMP) transaction monitoring
Integrated ICP canister queries for yield-to-Bitcoin routing calculations
Coordinated 100% of frontend development using agentic AI chat workflows
context: Satsuma was created at the Cosmoverse Hackathon to address the complexity of routing yield across disparate ecosystems (Cosmos/Injective, Axelar, and ICP).
The frontend coordinates multiple asynchronous state transitions across chains to ensure transactions don't get stuck without visual feedback.
problemSpace: High friction and lack of visibility in multi-chain transactions, leading to user anxiety during cross-chain asset routing
Complexity of aggregating data from Injective indexers, Astroport pools, and ICP canisters concurrently
Managing state and transaction signatures across Cosmos (Keplr) and ICP (Internet Identity) wallets simultaneously
approach: Model cross-chain transition stages as an animated, step-by-step progress tracker that updates reactively as contract messages are broadcast
Abstract multi-chain wallet status into a unified provider that updates the interface based on active chain context
outcomes: Successfully delivered the working cross-chain yield routing prototype during the 48-hour Cosmoverse Hackathon
Proven feasibility of directing complex cross-chain frontend integrations through agentic engineering flows while maintaining product quality

projects.title: Personal Projects
projects.content: Independent web systems delivered outside DeFi, including marketplace and business websites with production deployments and ongoing maintenance.
projects.content2: Representative work includes World Antiques and Collectables and Dungarvan Creek.
scope: Built and maintained client commercial websites
Designed SEO-optimized landing structures and CMS content pipes
Maintained continuous deployment cycles via Vercel
context: Delivered web systems for local businesses, focusing on rapid page loads, clean responsive design, and solid contact conversions.
problemSpace: Small business websites need very fast page speed and intuitive navigation to minimize bounce rates
Low-maintenance content management solutions for non-technical administrators
approach: Leverage Next.js static page generation and dynamic routing
Optimize images and load paths, ensuring high core web vitals
outcomes: Deployed and maintained production applications for World Antiques and Dungarvan Creek
Substantially increased search engine visibility and user conversions via semantic layout and custom styling

projects.title: Dyve
projects.roleDescriptor: Frontend Developer. Built the NFTFi lending, borrowing, and shorting interface
projects.content: Frontend developer for Dyve, a decentralized NFT lending, renting, and shorting platform developed for HackFS 2022. Created a brand new NFTFi primitive that allows users to safely lend, borrow, and short NFTs to unlock liquidity and profit from market downturns.
projects.content2: Designed and built the entire user interface and client architecture in React/Next.js and Tailwind CSS. Built custom Web3 integrations using Ethers.js and Web3React to facilitate peer-to-peer escrow setup, collateral deposits, and loan duration triggers directly with the Solidity contract suite.
projects.content3: Leveraged NFTPort and Covalent APIs to index collections and query wallet balances securely. Integrated IPFS metadata analysis to enable lenders to set trait-specific conditions, giving them control over exact assets, traits, or collection-wide floor parameters. Awarded the Covalent Best Use Prize, the NFTPort Pool Prize, and ranked in the Top 5 projects on Polygon at HackFS 2022.
projects.stack: Next.js, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, Ethers.js, Solidity
scope: Designed and built the full Next.js/Tailwind frontend application
Implemented clean dashboards for active NFT lending pools and borrow durations
Integrated Web3 wallet states, transaction broadcasts, and contract triggers
Developed custom IPFS metadata integrations for trait-specific lending rules
context: Dyve was created to solve the lack of hedging and yield-generation options for NFT holders during market downturns.
The project was awarded the Covalent Best Use Prize, the NFTPort Pool Prize, and ranked in the Top 5 projects on Polygon at HackFS 2022.
problemSpace: Millions of unproductive, idle NFTs in wallets without active yield-generation opportunities
Lack of financial tooling to hedge floor prices or short specific collections during bear markets
Vulnerability to scammers and stolen NFTs interacting with DeFi smart contracts
approach: Model borrow, lend, and short actions as explicit multi-stage transaction states in the UI
Utilize IPFS to query and verify trait-specific conditions selected by the lender
Integrate Covalent and NFTPort to verify NFT provenance and security boundaries
outcomes: Successfully shipped the fully interactive MVP at HackFS 2022
Won three sponsor prizes (Polygon Top 5, Covalent Best Use, NFTPort Pool Prize)
Delivered a secure frontend mapping peer-to-peer escrow parameters directly to smart contract calls

projects.title: CrAIdle
projects.roleDescriptor: Design Engineer / Agent Orchestrator. Directed autonomous agentic engineering flow
projects.content: Lead engineer and agent orchestrator for CrAIdle, a self-sustaining AI agent token launchpad and incubation treasury primitive developed for the Synthesis Hackathon. Enabled hatched AI agents to launch their own ERC-20 tokens on Base, with automated revenue streams flowing from trading fees directly back to their budgets.
projects.content2: Designed and deployed a yield-budgeted treasury model: user principal is deposited into a locked incubator vault, remaining protected and non-spendable, while generated yield (via Lido stETH) is dynamically routed to agent compute wallets. This separation of capital protects investor principal while sustaining long agent incubation cycles.
projects.content3: Directed a novel development pipeline, producing the entire Solidity contract suite and reactive Next.js/Tailwind gameboard-style interface entirely through autonomous orchestrated AI agents using the OpenClaw agent framework and MiniMax model platform. Deployed live on Base Sepolia with active wallet integrations.
projects.stack: Next.js, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, OpenClaw, MiniMax, Solidity
projects.domain: AI Agents / Token Launchpad / Yield Treasuries
scope: Designed and built the yield-budgeted treasury and token factory smart contracts
Orchestrated autonomous coding agent pipelines (OpenClaw + MiniMax) for code implementation
Designed a premium dark-mode gameboard dashboard interface for agent monitoring
Implemented Base Sepolia smart contract wallet connectivity and live data feeds
context: CrAIdle was created to solve runway fragility in autonomous AI agent systems where compute spend depletes investor principal.
The project demonstrates a fully self-building agent workflow, where all code was successfully generated, tested, and optimized by autonomous agents.
problemSpace: Compute costs drain agent capital runway rapidly, leading to premature termination of agent systems
No native mechanism exists to partition protected investment principal from operational compute spend
Visualizing multi-step incubation states, graduation thresholds, and yield budgeting is highly complex
approach: Design an Incubator Vault that locks principal and splits yield to a compute budget contract
Utilize OpenClaw autonomous agents and MiniMax models to generate, refine, and deploy frontend and Solidity code
Model agent incubation phases (Incubating, Graduated) as explicit, real-time visual trackers on a dark-themed cyberpunk dashboard
outcomes: Successfully deployed fully functional AgentToken, AgentTokenFactory, IncubatorVault, and GraduationManager contracts on Base Sepolia
Shipped a highly responsive, custom-designed Next.js/Tailwind gameboard-style interface for agent creation and monitoring
Proven zero-shot/few-shot agent code orchestration: 100% of codebase produced and optimized by AI agents

projects.title: Monad Heartbeat
projects.roleDescriptor: Frontend Developer / Designer. Built a real-time 3D node and transaction visualizer
projects.content: Lead developer and designer for Monad Heartbeat, a real-time block explorer and transaction lifecycle visualization tool for the Monad blockchain. Displays block progression from speculative mempool states to consensus, execution, and finality.
projects.content2: Built using Next.js, React, and Tailwind CSS, featuring mouse-responsive 3D block animations with custom perspective transforms. Integrated WebSocket support with fallback HTTP polling for continuous connection health indicators.
projects.content3: Designed a responsive transaction builder allowing users to construct and submit transaction payloads directly through EVM wallet interfaces, complete with connection monitoring and chain state feedback.
projects.stack: Next.js, React, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, WebSockets, Ethers.js
scope: Designed and implemented interactive 3D block components with CSS transform perspectives
Configured real-time block tracking and status indicators via WebSocket connections
Integrated EVM wallet adapters and built a custom payload transaction builder
Engineered automatic HTTP polling recovery for connection stability
context: Monad Heartbeat was created to provide an interactive, high-fidelity visualization of Monad's fast block times and transaction pipeline.
The application models the lifecycle of block states (Speculative, Executing, Consensus, Finalized) to make blockchain state changes tangible.
problemSpace: Visualizing sub-second block times and complex transaction states can feel static or confusing on traditional explorers
Maintaining reliable connection states with high-frequency WebSocket streams requires robust client-side socket managers
approach: Model blocks as mouse-responsive 3D cubes whose color and animation state change based on blockchain consensus status
Separate connection handling into a unified hook that manages socket state and falls back to JSON-RPC polling when needed
outcomes: Shipped a real-time visualization dashboard and transaction builder to Monad developers and community members
Delivered smooth 3D CSS rendering and reliable connection state UI across mobile and desktop browsers

projects.title: ether.fi Cash Demo
projects.roleDescriptor: Frontend Developer. Developed a high-fidelity interactive DeFi credit card dashboard
projects.content: Frontend developer for ether.fi Cash Demo, an interactive frontend application demonstrating the ether.fi Cash credit card product.
projects.content2: Designed a custom dark-mode dashboard that enables users to manage collateral (ETH, weETH, USDC) in liquid vaults, calculate borrowing capabilities, and simulate real-world card transaction streams with instant feedback.
projects.content3: Implemented responsive layouts and sleek interactive animations using React, Radix UI, TanStack Query, and Tailwind CSS, including a 3D perspective credit card mockup and interactive spending/credit controls.
projects.stack: Next.js, React, Radix UI, Tailwind CSS, TanStack Query
scope: Built the interactive dashboard layout and sidebar navigation structures
Developed a 3D responsive credit card visual card-details panel with perspective controls
Created vault deposit and borrowing calculators integrating Liquid Vault yield metrics
Implemented custom spending limits and security settings state management
context: ether.fi Cash is a DeFi-native credit card that allows cardholders to spend or borrow against their crypto balance in real-world environments.
The demo highlights the user experience of managing staked assets while maintaining spending power.
problemSpace: Explaining borrowing boundaries and liquidation risks for collateralized credit cards in a clean, visual manner
Structuring responsive dashboard panels that load heavy mock transaction data smoothly without UI stuttering
approach: Provide a high-fidelity interactive dashboard that mimics real mobile and desktop credit card applications
Implement real-time balance calculations as users adjust deposit sliders, demonstrating direct effects of interest accrual
outcomes: Delivered a fully interactive card management and collateral dashboard demo, showcasing ether.fi Cash features
Shipped smooth page transitions, custom light/dark theme components, and responsive spending limit panels
